_cite.reason: URL-normalization 1c: strip trailing markdown-emphasis `*`/backtick glued to citation URLs in intelligence_item_sections.content_md (customer-checkable link + validate_item_provenance criterion-2 exact-compare). Operator ruling 2026-07-04. Same transform as the write-site fix.

prior.content_md:
EPA's HD GHG Phase 3 rule (40 CFR parts 86, 1036, 1037, 1039, 1054, and 1065) imposes compliance obligations exclusively on vehicle **manufacturers and engine makers** — the entities that design, certify, and sell new Class 2b–8 heavy-duty highway vehicles. The rule is a production/sales standard, not an operational standard for fleet operators, freight forwarders, or logistics intermediaries.

As a freight forwarder, the workspace sits downstream of the regulated manufacturer tier. It neither manufactures vehicles nor holds EPA certificates of conformity. Accordingly, the workspace bears **no direct registration, reporting, or certification obligation** under Phase 3 as currently written.

However, the workspace is operationally exposed in two indirect ways. First, as a **purchaser or lessor of road transport capacity**, the workspace's carrier and subcontractor panel will be shaped by what vehicle manufacturers produce and at what price premium. The rule's technology-neutral, performance-based approach means carriers may adopt any mix of advanced ICE, hybrid, PHEV, BEV, or hydrogen fuel cell powertrains to comply — a procurement diversity the workspace must track when evaluating carrier cost stability and asset reliability for time-critical, high-value cargo (live events equipment, fine art, humanitarian relief). Second, as a **scope-3 emissions reporter** for shipper clients who are themselves subject to sustainability disclosure requirements, the workspace may need to document the GHG intensity of contracted road moves, particularly on US domestic lanes where MY 2027+ vehicle specifications directly affect per-ton-mile CO2 performance.

Role placement requiring legal confirmation: Whether the workspace, acting as a shipper-of-record on domestic US intermodal moves (air to road last-mile) constitutes a "purchaser" for purposes of any EPA fleet-average incentive programmes or future complementary rules should be confirmed with US regulatory counsel.

*Source: Greenhouse Gas Emissions Standards for Heavy-Duty Vehicles—Phase 3, EPA, 89 FR 29440, April 22, 2024. https://federalregister.gov/documents/2024/04/22/2024-06809/greenhouse-gas-emissions-standards-for-heavy-duty-vehicles-phase-3. EPA Final Rule landing page, EPA, March 2024. https://www.epa.gov/regulations-emissions-vehicles-and-engines/final-rule-greenhouse-gas-emissions-standards-heavy-duty.*
